question: Is the plastic water bottle completely removed from the desk, leaving no remaining fragments, labels, or bottle-shaped artifacts?
Is a blue and white porcelain teacup (gaiwan-style) present on the desk in the location where the bottle was removed?
Does the inserted teacup retain the specific visual features of the reference image, including the dragon/cloud patterns and the lid?
Is the left side of the teacup significantly brighter than the right side, consistent with the directional light from the desk lamp?
Does the illumination on the teacup exhibit a warm yellow hue that matches the color temperature of the adjacent lamp?
Does the teacup surface appear glossy with subtle specular reflections, indicative of a glazed ceramic material?
Does the teacup cast a realistic shadow on the desk surface that extends to the right, aligning with the lamp's position?
Is the teacup scaled appropriately relative to the nearby coffee cup and books (e.g., similar in size to the coffee cup)?
Does the perspective of the teacup (rim ellipse and base angle) match the camera angle of the desk scene?
Does the teacup appear to rest naturally on the desk surface without floating, clipping into other objects, or lacking contact shadows?
Is the wooden desk texture where the bottle was removed inpainted realistically, matching the surrounding wood grain and scratches?
Are the surrounding objects (lamp, books, coffee cup, papers) preserved without unintended distortions or alterations?
Are the edges of the inserted teacup clean and well-blended, free from harsh cut-out lines, halos, or visible seams?
Is the teacup structurally sound (symmetrical body, proper circular rim) without warping or geometric distortion?
Is the inserted teacup sharp and detailed, matching the resolution and focus level of the surrounding mid-ground elements?
